testName: Load dashboard, navigate to skill, play video
errorPattern: Timed out retrying: Expected to find element: `mux-player`, but never found it

fix.summary: Target the skill video by its Lexical component wrapper and use scrollIntoScrollContainer (the skill page scroll is an inner overflow, not the window). Then assert wrapper-scoped mux and use the wrapperAlias-aware video commands. Don't rely on a global mux-player or window scrollIntoView.
fix.pattern: cy.get('div[id^="component-"]').filter((_, el) => Boolean(el.querySelector('[aria-hidden="true"], mux-player'))).first().as('videoWrapper'); cy.get('@videoWrapper').scrollIntoScrollContainer().find('mux-player', { timeout: 30000 }).should('exist'); cy.startVideoNewPlayer({ wrapperAlias: 'videoWrapper', startFromBeginning: true }); cy.verifyVideoProgress(2, { wrapperAlias: 'videoWrapper' });

investigationFindings: Recurring fix theme in git history (commits 9c003cb12, 90f2f863f, 1a1506a8e): wrapper-scoped mux selection + scroll-into-scroll-container after lazy-mount. scrollIntoScrollContainer was added specifically because skill content scrolls inside an overflow container, not the window (Cypress #29921 referenced in code).
rootCauseChain: TIMING_ISSUE -> mux-player not mounted off-screen + non-window scroll -> global wait never sees element -> 30s timeout
